approvalNote: Peter selected John of the North as closest on 2026-06-16, then asked to audition just him with different emotional tones and shorter, less tortured wording.

john_of_the_north.role: Gruff dry Yorkshire-ish interruption voice for JobDone enterprise parody

takes.speaker: yorkshireman
takes.text: No. Stop that. It's a job diary, lad. Finish the job, say what happened, check it once, and find it later. JobDone. Get it out of your head before you forget it.
takes.title: JobDone Yorkshire John Tone - Short Interrupt

takes.speaker: yorkshireman
takes.text: No. Stop that. It's not a platform. It's a job diary, lad. Revolutionary, I know. You say what happened before you drive off, then JobDone helps you find it later.
takes.title: JobDone Yorkshire John Tone - Dry Sarcastic

takes.speaker: yorkshireman
takes.text: No. Stop that. Fancy words won't fix a leaky tap. JobDone is a job diary. Say what happened, check it, and find it later when your head's full.
takes.title: JobDone Yorkshire John Tone - Weary Deadpan

takes.speaker: yorkshireman
takes.text: No. Stop that. JobDone is a job diary for trades. Finish a job, say what happened, confirm it, and ask for it next time you're there.
takes.title: JobDone Yorkshire John Tone - Gruff Practical

takes.speaker: yorkshireman
takes.text: No. Stop that. If it takes three breaths to explain, it's not helping. JobDone lets you say the job, confirm it, and find it later.
takes.title: JobDone Yorkshire John Tone - Wry Old Hand

takes.speaker: yorkshireman
takes.text: No. Stop that. It's a job diary, lad. Not a boardroom chant. You finish the work, speak the facts, confirm them, and find them when you need them.
takes.title: JobDone Yorkshire John Tone - Does Not Suffer Fools
